overall_cve.delta_vs_prior_run: April MTD moved 5083 (day 26) → 5174 (day 27): +91 CVEs net over 24h. Extrapolation moved 5865 → 5748 (-2.0%). Day-over-day rate (91 CVE/day) is below the running April average (~192/day) and consistent with continued Sunday-tail churn (NVD assignments came in Sunday afternoon US time). Week-17 (Apr 20-26) closed at 1469 (was 1399 partial). Week-18 (Apr 27-May 03) opens at 21 entries on day 1. Counter-argument: extrapolation will tighten as the remaining 3 days resolve and NVD backfills late publications; even at 5748, April lands meaningfully below March (6304) and consistent with the Q1 churn band (4808-6304). No positive evidence of a Glasswing-driven volume shock. Conversely, NVD publication latency of 4-8 weeks means Mythos disclosures from April may not appear in NVD until mid-May at earliest.

exploit_intelligence.watch_list_status_summary: 14 watch-list CVEs total. Maturity unchanged from 2026-04-25T04:05Z full scan: 5 weaponized (CVE-2026-39987, -33825, -32201, -34197, -34621), 1 functional (CVE-2026-21858 n8n), 2 PoC (CVE-2026-40477, -40478 Thymeleaf), 6 none (wolfSSL pair + Cisco quartet). 5 of 14 (36%) have public exploit code or weaponized status. No newly-escalated entries in the last 24h. No incremental web searches run today (low-signal Sunday cadence; next full sweep scheduled 2026-04-28).
new_observations: VulnCheck (Patrick Garrity) baseline aligns with our tracking: 40 Anthropic-attributed CVEs across 5 vendors. No new disclosures in the last 24h.

CVE-2026-6951 (simple-git CWE-78 OS command injection, CVSS 9.8) is making the rounds today as a CI/CD library RCE. Not on our watch list — simple-git is a CLI wrapper, not a network parser; the bug requires untrusted input reaching the options arg, which is more an integration-pattern issue than a wire-format parser flaw. Recording as monitor-only NP+DI candidate.
CVE-2026-5760 (SGLang RCE via malicious GGUF model files) also published today (CVSS 9.8). Not NP+DI — model file parser, not network parser. Skip.
exploit_intelligence.summary: 5 of 14 watch list CVEs have public exploits or weaponized status. 0 newly escalated since prior run. Last full ExploitDB/Metasploit/GitHub PoC sweep: 2026-04-25T04:05Z. Next due: 2026-04-28.

np_di_candidates.np_component: git CLI wrapper — option-parsing bypass via --config alias
np_di_candidates.rationale: Borderline NP — simple-git is a CLI wrapper not a wire-format parser. Bug requires untrusted input reaching the options argument (CI/CD with attacker-controlled URLs). Useful as a CI/CD-supply-chain example but not a tier-1 network-parser exposure.

np_di_candidates.cwe: unknown (likely deserialization or path traversal in GGUF loader)
np_di_candidates.rationale: Not NP+DI in the http-parser sense — model file parser, not network parser. Skip for watch list.

projection_comparison.note: Both conservative and aggressive scenarios were anchored to 5865 in the prior run. Today's extrapolation (5748) lands ~2% below that anchor. Re-anchored to 5748. Counter-argument: NVD publication lag means actual April content will continue to grow over the next 4-8 weeks; the extrapolation is a lower bound. The cumulative pattern of below-conservative monthly extrapolations across April still gives no positive evidence of an AI-driven CVE-volume inflection — but this is consistent with NVD-lag eating any signal that exists. Forward expectations: May 2026 may show the first 'AI surge' if it's coming, since April-disclosed Mythos finds will start appearing in NVD assignments mid-to-late May.
